name: SBI Miles Elite Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Spend ₹5,00,000 or more in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 10 Air Miles per ₹100 on all international transactions
4 Air Miles per ₹100 on domestic travel bookings (flights, hotels, cabs)
2 Air Miles per ₹100 on all other domestic transactions

redemptionOptions: Transfer to airline frequent flyer programs (Vistara, Singapore Airlines, etc.)
Flight bookings via SBI Card portal

welcomeBenefits.condition: On spending ₹10,000 within 60 days of card issuance

milestoneBenefits.benefit: Additional 10,000 bonus Air Miles + annual fee waiver

additionalBenefits: Low foreign currency markup of 1.99% — ideal for international use
Transferable miles to multiple airline partners
8 domestic + 4 international lounge visits per year
Travel insurance with ₹50 lakh emergency medical cover
pros: 10 miles per ₹100 on international spends is excellent — effective 5% return
Low forex markup of 1.99% makes it one of the best cards for international use
8 domestic + 4 international lounge visits provide strong travel coverage
₹1 crore air accident insurance and ₹50 lakh travel insurance
Welcome bonus of 10,000 miles covers the joining fee value
cons: High annual fee of ₹4,999 with ₹5 lakh waiver threshold
Base earn rate of 2 miles per ₹100 is average for everyday domestic spends
Mile value of ₹0.50 is modest compared to some airline co-branded cards
High income eligibility of ₹12 lakh limits accessibility
idealFor: Frequent international travelers who want low forex markup and high miles earning
Travel enthusiasts who want transferable airline miles with flexibility
Users with high travel spends looking for comprehensive travel insurance and lounge access

faqs.question: How much are the Air Miles worth?
faqs.answer: Each Air Mile is worth approximately ₹0.50 when redeemed as statement credit. However, value can be higher when transferred to airline frequent flyer programs for flight bookings, especially during sales.

faqs.question: Can I transfer miles to airline programs?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can transfer Air Miles to partner airline frequent flyer programs including Vistara Club Vistara and Singapore Airlines KrisFlyer, among others.

faqs.question: What makes this card good for international use?
faqs.answer: The combination of 10 miles per ₹100 on international spends (5% effective return) and a low 1.99% forex markup makes this one of the best SBI cards for international transactions.

faqs.question: What insurance coverage does this card provide?
faqs.answer: The card offers ₹1 crore air accident insurance and up to ₹50 lakh emergency medical travel insurance when tickets are purchased using the card.
